CheckTitle: AI Agent API Access Must Route Through an Authenticated API Gateway
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent routes all API access through an authenticated API Gateway rather than connecting directly to backend services. An API Gateway provides a centralised enforcement point for authentication, authorisation, rate limiting, and audit logging.
Risk: Direct backend access bypasses all API Gateway controls. An attacker or a prompt-injected agent that discovers a direct backend URL can interact with services without authentication or rate limiting, and these calls will not appear in API Gateway logs.

Code.Other:
1. Set api_gateway_enforced: true in the agent manifest.
2. Configure backend services to accept requests only from the API Gateway's IP range or VPC endpoint.
3. Use security groups, VPC service endpoints, or private link to block direct internet access to backend APIs.

Recommendation.Text: Route all agent API calls through a centralised, authenticated API Gateway. Block direct access to backend services at the network layer using security groups or private link configurations.

Notes: ASPM-042 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
